codeReview.summary: The circuit breaker implementation has two blocking bugs: (1) the automatic Open→HalfOpen transition never occurs because `check()` returns Ok without calling `transition_to()`, and (2) `record_success()` in Open state has inconsistent statistics handling. Additionally, the public API types are not exported from lib.rs, limiting usability.

issues.description: BUG: When in Open state and recovery timeout has elapsed, `check()` returns `Ok(())` but does NOT call `transition_to(CircuitState::HalfOpen)`. The state remains `Open`, so subsequent `record_failure()` or `record_success()` calls will be handled as Open state instead of HalfOpen state. This breaks automatic recovery - the circuit never actually transitions to HalfOpen. Fix: Add `self.transition_to(CircuitState::HalfOpen);` before `return Ok(());` in the Open branch.

BUG: In `record_success()` when in Open state (lines 224-227), the code decrements `total_requests` and `total_successes` to undo the earlier increment, but does NOT decrement `total_failures`. This causes inconsistent statistics. The fix should also decrement `total_failures`.

Missing public exports: The circuit breaker types (CircuitBreaker, CircuitState, CircuitBreakerConfig, CircuitBreakerError, CircuitBreakerSnapshot) are not re-exported from lib.rs, so external crates cannot easily import them. Other modules like kill_switch have `pub use kill_switch::{...}` but circuit_breaker only has `pub mod circuit_breaker;`. Should add `pub use circuit_breaker::{CircuitBreaker, CircuitBreakerConfig, CircuitBreakerError, CircuitBreakerSnapshot, CircuitState};`

Comment inconsistency: The comment says 'Already open, just reset the timeout' but the code doesn't actually reset any timeout. The `last_state_change_at` is not updated in the Open→Open case. Either update the comment or update the code.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The Rust skill file was not found at .factory/skills/Rust/SKILL.md. Transcript shows worker read mission.md, validation-contract.md, and existing source files, then created circuit_breaker.rs. The skill guidance was not consulted or may be missing.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The verification step in features.json says `cargo test -p swell-orchestrator -- circuit_breaker` but the circuit breaker tests are actually in `swell-core`. The verification technically passes because cargo test doesn't fail when no tests match, but this is misleading. Should be `cargo test -p swell-core -- circuit_breaker`.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: features.json shows verificationSteps: ["cargo build --workspace", "cargo test -p swell-orchestrator -- circuit_breaker"] but tests are in crates/swell-core/src/circuit_breaker.rs

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker used only standard cargo commands (cargo build, cargo test) which are already in AGENTS.md. No additional services were started.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Verification commands in handoff show only cargo build --workspace and cargo test commands

summary: The circuit breaker feature has TWO BLOCKING bugs that prevent automatic recovery from working correctly. First, the `check()` method returns Ok after the recovery timeout but does not actually transition the state to HalfOpen, leaving the circuit stuck in Open state. Second, `record_success()` has inconsistent statistics handling when called in Open state. These bugs cause the automatic recovery mechanism to be non-functional. The feature needs fixing before it can be considered complete.
